channel.title: CyberNetSec - Latest Cybersecurity Articles
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity threat intelligence articles from the past 3 days

item.title: Pro-Iranian Group 'Handala' Claims 'Warning' Attack on California Water Systems
item.description:
The pro-Iranian hacktivist group 'Handala' has claimed responsibility for a cyberattack against California water infrastructure. The group framed the intrusion as a retaliatory 'warning' to the U.S. for alleged strikes on Iranian water resources. While Handala asserted it did not disrupt water suppl...

Severity: High

Categories: Cyberattack, Threat Actor, Industrial Control Systems

Read Full Article →

item.title: Conti Ransomware Coder Pleads Guilty in U.S. Court for Wire Fraud Conspiracy
item.description:
Oleksii Oleksiyovych Lytvynenko, a 44-year-old Ukrainian national, has pleaded guilty in a U.S. federal court to conspiracy to commit wire fraud. He admitted to his role in the notorious Conti ransomware gang, which was responsible for over $150 million in ransom payments. Lytvynenko, who was arrest...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Actor, Security Operations

Read Full Article →

item.title: 'Atomic Arch' Supply Chain Attack Hijacks Orphaned Linux Packages to Target Developers
item.description:
A sophisticated supply chain attack dubbed 'Atomic Arch' is targeting Linux developers by hijacking trusted but orphaned packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR). Attackers claim ownership of these abandoned packages and modify their build scripts (PKGBUILD) to fetch and install a malicious npm pa...

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: FinCEN Issues New Guidance to Encourage Banks to Share Fraud-Related Information
item.description:
The U.S. Treasury's Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) has released updated guidance to encourage financial institutions to voluntarily share information related to suspected fraud. The guidance clarifies that the safe harbor provisions of Section 314(b) of the USA PATRIOT Act apply to th...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: White House Overhauls Cybersecurity Policy for National Security Systems with NSPM-12
item.description:
On June 12, 2026, President Donald J. Trump signed National Security Presidential Memorandum (NSPM-12), a new directive to overhaul the cybersecurity governance for America's most sensitive National Security Systems (NSS). The memo re-establishes the Committee on National Security Systems (CNSS) aft...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Security Operations

Read Full Article →

item.title: Novo Nordisk Confirms Data Breach, Urges Vigilance from Trial Participants
item.description:
Danish pharmaceutical company Novo Nordisk announced on June 11, 2026, that it suffered a cybersecurity incident where attackers gained unauthorized access to internal IT systems. The company confirmed that personal data related to some clinical trial participants was accessed. While Novo Nordisk st...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Data Breach, Cyberattack, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: Canadian Regulator Warns of Risks from Advanced AI in Cybersecurity
item.description:
The Canadian Investment Regulatory Organization (CIRO) has issued a cybersecurity alert to its members regarding the dual-use nature of frontier Artificial Intelligence (AI) models. The bulletin, published on June 11, 2026, warns that while advanced AI can enhance cyber defenses, it also significant...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: DXC and Anthropic Form Alliance to Deploy AI in Enterprise Systems
item.description:
IT services giant DXC Technology and AI safety company Anthropic have announced a major global partnership. The multi-year alliance will focus on integrating Anthropic's Claude AI models into the complex, mission-critical systems DXC manages for its enterprise clients in sectors like banking, insura...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: DHS Cyber Modernization Efforts Face Hurdles, GAO Report Finds
item.description:
A new report from the U.S. Government Accountability Office (GAO) finds that while major cybersecurity programs at the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) are strengthening federal cyber defenses, they face significant hurdles. The June 12, 2026 report highlights challenges related to mounting cos...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Security Operations

Read Full Article →

item.title: Global Schools Foundation Suffers Major Data Breach, Exposing 33,000 Passports
item.description:
The Global Schools Foundation (GSF), an international K-12 education organization, has been impacted by a significant data breach attributed to the threat actor 'FulcrumSec'. The incident, reported on June 11, 2026, resulted in the exposure of highly sensitive personal information of both students a...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: Team Cymru Expands Asia-Pacific Operations to Meet Threat Intel Demand
item.description:
Threat intelligence firm Team Cymru has announced a major expansion of its operations in the Asia-Pacific and Japan (APJ) region, naming Sydney, Australia, as its new regional hub. The move, announced on June 12, 2026, is driven by increasing demand from critical infrastructure, financial services, ...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Security Operations

Read Full Article →

item.title: Ontinue Awarded 'Most Innovative XDR Platform' for AI-Driven Security
item.description:
Ontinue, a provider of Managed Extended Detection and Response (MXDR), has won the 'Most Innovative XDR Platform' award in the 2026 Cybersecurity Stars Awards by The Hacker News. The award, announced on June 12, 2026, recognizes Ontinue's ION platform and its 'Agentic SOC' model. This model is desig...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: VRChat Cloud Breach Exposes Data of 2.4 Million Users, Including Login History and Linked Accounts
item.description:
The social virtual reality platform VRChat has disclosed a data breach that exposed the personal information of 2,436,782 users. The incident, which occurred in May 2026, resulted from an unauthorized third party gaining access to the company's cloud environment. Stolen data includes usernames, emai...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Vietnam's OceanLotus APT Pivots to Domestic Spying, Hits Construction and Finance Sectors
item.description:
The Vietnam-aligned threat group OceanLotus (also known as APT32) has shifted its focus to domestic espionage, targeting a Vietnamese construction firm and stock market investors. According to research from ESET, the group conducted a year-long intrusion and a sophisticated supply-chain attack compr...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Supply Chain Attack, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: CISA Mandates Risk-Based Patching for Federal Agencies with New Directive BOD 26-04
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 26-04, fundamentally changing how federal civilian agencies must manage cybersecurity vulnerabilities. The new directive, effective June 10, 2026, mandates a risk-based prioritization mode...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: Researcher Drops 'GreatXML' Zero-Day Exploit to Bypass Windows BitLocker
item.description:
A security researcher known as Chaotic Eclipse has publicly released details of 'GreatXML,' a new unpatched zero-day exploit that allegedly bypasses Windows BitLocker encryption. The exploit allows an attacker with physical access to a machine to gain SYSTEM-level privileges in the Windows Recovery ...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: DragonForce Ransomware Gang Claims Attack on Taos Mountain Casino, Stealing SSNs
item.description:
Taos Mountain Casino in New Mexico has confirmed it was the victim of a ransomware attack that resulted in a data breach. The incident, which occurred in late March 2026, was claimed by the DragonForce ransomware group on June 1. The gang alleges it stole 38.63 GB of data, including the names, addre...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Data Breach

Read Full Article →

item.title: South Korea Hits E-Commerce Giant Coupang with Record $409M Fine Over Data Breach
item.description:
South Korea's data privacy regulator, the Personal Information Protection Commission (PIPC), has levied a historic fine of 624.68 billion won (approx. $409 million) against e-commerce giant Coupang. The penalty stems from a data breach affecting 37.55 million people, which the regulator attributed t...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Regulatory, Policy and Compliance

Read Full Article →

item.title: Jamaica's National Health Fund Probes Cyberattack as Hackers Claim Patient Data Theft
item.description:
Jamaica's National Health Fund (NHF) is conducting an active investigation into a cybersecurity incident after being contacted by a hacker group. The unidentified group claims to have breached the NHF's systems and stolen highly confidential patient data, including medication records and beneficiary...

Severity: High

Categories: Cyberattack, Data Breach, Industrial Control Systems

Read Full Article →

item.title: Oracle Rushes Emergency Patch for PeopleSoft Zero-Day Exploited by ShinyHunters
item.description:
Oracle has released an emergency, out-of-band patch for a critical unauthenticated remote code execution (RCE) zero-day vulnerability in its PeopleSoft PeopleTools software, tracked as CVE-2026-35273. The urgent release follows confirmation that the flaw is being actively exploited in the wild by th...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Actor, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Russian APTs Persistently Exploit Year-Old WinRAR Flaw in Attacks on Ukraine
item.description:
A high-severity vulnerability in WinRAR, CVE-2025-8088, patched nearly a year ago, is still being actively exploited by multiple Russian-linked APT groups against targets in Ukraine. According to Trend Micro, the Gamaredon (UAC-0010) and SHADOW-EARTH-066 (UAC-0226) groups are using the flaw to deliv...

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Actor, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Europol Busts 'AudiA6' Crypto Laundering Service Used by Ransomware Gangs
item.description:
An international law enforcement operation led by Europol has taken down 'AudiA6,' a major cryptocurrency laundering service that allegedly washed over €336 million for cybercriminals, primarily ransomware gangs, between 2022 and 2025. The operation resulted in arrests in Georgia, the seizure of ove...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Ransomware, Policy and Compliance

Read Full Article →

item.title: AI Agent 'Skills' Pose Major Supply Chain Risk; New Audit Tool Finds 80% Deviate from Declared Behavior
item.description:
A new study by Unit 42 reveals significant supply chain risks within the burgeoning AI agent ecosystem. Researchers developed a new audit primitive, Behavioral Integrity Verification (BIV), to analyze third-party "skills" used by Large Language Model (LLM) agents. A scan of the OpenClaw registry fou...

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Threat Intelligence, Malware

Read Full Article →

item.title: Microsoft's Record-Breaking June Patch Tuesday: Over 200 Flaws and Three Zero-Days Patched
item.description:
Microsoft has released its largest-ever Patch Tuesday for June 2026, addressing a staggering 200 vulnerabilities across its product ecosystem. The update includes fixes for 33 critical flaws and three publicly disclosed zero-day vulnerabilities. Among the most severe are a wormable Windows Kernel RC...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Patch Management, Vulnerability, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Energy Sector in Crosshairs: 66% of APT Campaigns Target Utilities, Report Finds
item.description:
A new intelligence report from CYFIRMA reveals a concentrated and sustained cyber espionage campaign against the global energy and utilities sector. Over the past three months, this critical infrastructure sector was the target in 66% of all observed Advanced Persistent Threat (APT) campaigns. The m...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Cyberattack, Industrial Control Systems

Read Full Article →

item.title: Ivanti Patches Critical Sentry Flaws Allowing Root-Level RCE
item.description:
Ivanti has released urgent security updates for two critical vulnerabilities in its Ivanti Sentry (formerly MobileIron Sentry) product. The most severe flaw, CVE-2026-10520, is an unauthenticated OS command injection vulnerability that allows a remote attacker to achieve root-level remote code execu...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Mobile Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Ransomware Attack on Illinois High School Disables Safety Systems, Forcing Campus Shutdown
item.description:
A ransomware attack on June 7, 2026, forced Evanston Township High School (ETHS) in Illinois to shut down its campus for two days. The cyberattack had a severe physical impact, disabling critical building safety systems including door access controls and public address systems, rendering the school ...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Cyberattack, Industrial Control Systems

Read Full Article →

item.title: Wave of Data Breaches Hits Global Firms as Multiple Threat Actors Strike
item.description:
June 10, 2026, saw a flurry of data breach announcements with multiple threat actor groups claiming responsibility for attacks on a diverse set of global companies. The victims span telecommunications, IT, manufacturing, logistics, and finance. U.S. telecom company AireSpring was allegedly hit by 'C...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Threat Actor, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: ICS Patch Tuesday: Siemens, Schneider, Phoenix Contact Release Critical Advisories
item.description:
The June 2026 Industrial Control Systems (ICS) Patch Tuesday featured important security advisories from major OT vendors including Siemens, Schneider Electric, and Phoenix Contact. Siemens released four advisories covering multiple flaws in Sinec INS, Siprotec 5 relays, and other products, includin...

Severity: High

Categories: Industrial Control Systems, Vulnerability, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: LockBit 5.0 Ransomware Gang Claims Attack on Singapore's SCB Group
item.description:
The notorious LockBit 5.0 ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation has claimed responsibility for a cyberattack against SCB Group, a construction company based in Singapore. The claim was posted on the group's dark web leak site on June 9, 2026. In a classic double-extortion tactic, the threat actor...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Threat Actor, Data Breach

Read Full Article →
